nodes.label: Partner's basis in its interest
nodes.syn: Cash + adjusted basis of contributed property + share of liabilities, adjusted annually; never below zero.

nodes.label: Partnership's basis in its assets
nodes.syn: Carryover from contributions; adjustable under §743(b)/§734(b) only with a §754 election.

nodes.label: Economic-equity capital account
nodes.syn: Maintained under Treas. Reg. 1.704-1(b)(2)(iv); measures economic equity; may be negative; distinct from tax basis and FMV.

nodes.label: Recourse vs nonrecourse liability
nodes.syn: Recourse if a partner bears the economic risk of loss; nonrecourse if none does. A partner's share is part of outside basis under §752.

nodes.label: Unrealized receivables & inventory
nodes.syn: Unrealized receivables (§751(c)) and inventory (§751(d)); generate ordinary income on a sale of an interest or a disproportionate distribution.

nodes.label: Contribution that is in substance a sale
nodes.syn: A contribution plus a related transfer that in substance is a sale; tested under §707 and the 1.707 regs; two-year presumption.

nodes.label: Precontribution gain/loss
nodes.syn: Difference between FMV and adjusted basis of contributed property at contribution; allocated back to the contributing partner under §704(c).

nodes.label: Inside-basis adjustment election
nodes.syn: Election enabling inside-basis adjustments under §743(b) (transfers) and §734(b) (distributions); generally irrevocable.

nodes.label: Whether a partnership exists
nodes.syn: Whether an arrangement is a partnership for tax purposes; a facts-and-circumstances / intent inquiry.

nodes.label: Securities treated as money
nodes.syn: Treated as money under §731(c) for distribution gain, reduced by the partner's share of built-in gain in the securities.

nodes.label: Liability outside disguised-sale
nodes.syn: A liability (e.g., debt >2 years old encumbering contributed property) whose assumption does not by itself trigger the disguised-sale rules.

nodes.label: Nonrecognition on contribution
nodes.syn: No gain or loss on a contribution of property for a partnership interest (subject to exceptions).

nodes.label: Investment-company exception
nodes.syn: Nonrecognition does not apply to a contribution to an investment company that diversifies; built-in GAIN only.

nodes.label: Initial outside basis (contribution)
nodes.syn: Initial outside basis = cash contributed + adjusted basis of contributed property (+ §721(b) gain).

nodes.label: Initial inside basis (contribution)
nodes.syn: Partnership's basis in contributed property = the contributor's adjusted basis (carryover).

nodes.label: Character of contributed property
nodes.syn: Preserves ordinary/capital character of contributed receivables, inventory, and capital-loss property for stated periods.

nodes.label: Allocations / substantial economic effect
nodes.syn: Allocations are respected if they have substantial economic effect or match the partners' interests in the partnership.

nodes.label: Built-in gain allocations
nodes.syn: Built-in gain or loss on contributed property is allocated to the contributing partner.

nodes.label: Loss limitation
nodes.syn: A partner's distributive share of loss is allowed only to the extent of outside basis; the excess is suspended.

nodes.label: Charitable / FTC in basis limit (TCJA)
nodes.syn: Charitable contributions and foreign taxes are inside the §704(d) limit; donated built-in-gain property reduces basis only by the property's basis share.

nodes.label: Basis adjustment rules
nodes.syn: Umbrella rule for adjusting outside basis by income, contributions, liabilities, distributions, losses, and nondeductible items.

nodes.label: Increase: taxable income
nodes.syn: Outside basis increases by the partner's distributive share of taxable income, including capital gain.

nodes.label: Increase: tax-exempt income
nodes.syn: Outside basis increases by the partner's share of tax-exempt income.

nodes.label: Increase: excess depletion
nodes.syn: Outside basis increases by percentage depletion exceeding the basis of depletable property.

nodes.label: Decrease: losses
nodes.syn: Outside basis decreases by the partner's distributive share of losses, including capital loss.

nodes.label: Decrease: nondeductible expense
nodes.syn: Outside basis decreases by the partner's share of nondeductible, noncapital expenditures.

nodes.label: Decrease: oil & gas depletion
nodes.syn: Outside basis decreases by the partner's share of depletion on oil and gas property.

nodes.label: Alternative basis rule
nodes.syn: When the general rule is impractical, basis may be figured as the partner's share of the partnership's adjusted basis in its property.

nodes.label: Partner-partnership transactions
nodes.syn: Governs transactions between a partner and the partnership, including disguised sales and guaranteed payments.

nodes.label: Guaranteed payments
nodes.syn: Payments fixed without regard to income are ordinary income to the partner and generally deductible by the partnership.

nodes.label: Gain/loss on distribution
nodes.syn: A partner recognizes gain only if money distributed exceeds outside basis; no loss except in limited liquidations.

nodes.label: Gain on excess distribution
nodes.syn: Gain to the extent money (incl. marketable securities and liability relief) distributed exceeds outside basis; generally capital.

nodes.label: Loss on liquidation only
nodes.syn: No loss on a current distribution; loss only on liquidation receiving solely cash, receivables, and/or inventory.

nodes.label: Marketable securities as money
nodes.syn: Marketable securities are treated as money for distribution gain, reduced by the partner's share of built-in gain.

nodes.label: Basis of distributed property
nodes.syn: Distributed-property basis carries over, capped at outside basis less money (§732(a)(2)); liquidation takes remaining basis (§732(b)).

nodes.label: Basis reduction on distribution
nodes.syn: Outside basis is reduced (not below zero) by money distributed plus the basis of property distributed.

nodes.label: Inside-basis adjustment (distribution)
nodes.syn: Partnership adjusts inside basis on a distribution if a §754 election is in effect or there is a substantial basis reduction.

nodes.label: Retiring-partner payments
nodes.syn: Liquidation payments to a retiring or deceased partner split between property payments (§736(b)) and other payments (§736(a)).

nodes.label: Sale of a partnership interest
nodes.syn: Sale of an interest yields capital gain or loss equal to amount realized less outside basis, subject to §751.

nodes.label: Basis of purchased interest
nodes.syn: Initial outside basis of a purchased interest = cost (cash + property) plus share of liabilities.

nodes.label: Inside-basis adjustment (transfer)
nodes.syn: On a sale/exchange of an interest, with a §754 election or substantial built-in loss, inside basis is adjusted for the transferee.

nodes.label: Hot assets
nodes.syn: Recharacterizes as ordinary income the partner's share of unrealized receivables and inventory on a sale or disproportionate distribution.

nodes.label: Disproportionate distributions
nodes.syn: A distribution that shifts a partner's interest in hot assets is treated as a deemed sale producing ordinary income.

nodes.label: Treatment of liabilities
nodes.syn: A partner's share of liabilities is part of outside basis: an increase is a deemed contribution, a decrease a deemed distribution.

nodes.label: Liability increase = contribution
nodes.syn: An increase in a partner's share of liabilities is treated as a contribution of money, raising outside basis.

nodes.label: Liability decrease = distribution
nodes.syn: A decrease in a partner's share of liabilities is treated as a distribution of money, lowering outside basis.

nodes.label: Optional basis-adjustment election
nodes.syn: Election that turns on the inside-basis adjustments of §743(b) and §734(b); once made, generally irrevocable.

nodes.label: Allocation of basis adjustment
nodes.syn: Allocates a §743(b) or §734(b) adjustment among partnership assets by relative built-in gain and loss.

nodes.label: Precontribution gain on distribution
nodes.syn: A distribution to a partner who contributed 704(c) property within 7 years can trigger recognition of precontribution gain.

nodes.label: Organizational & syndication expenses
nodes.syn: Organizational expenses may be amortized; syndication expenses must be capitalized and are not amortizable.

nodes.label: Cost basis
nodes.syn: Basis of purchased property is its cost.

nodes.label: Investment-company definition
nodes.syn: Defines the investment-company concept imported into §721(b).

nodes.label: Business interest limitation
nodes.syn: Deductible business interest limited to interest income plus 30% of adjusted taxable income; OBBBA restored the more favorable EBITDA-based ATI for years after 2024.

nodes.label: Qualified business income deduction
nodes.syn: Up to a 20% deduction for qualified business income from a passthrough; made PERMANENT by OBBBA (P.L. 119-21), with expanded phase-in thresholds and a $400 minimum (199A(i)).

nodes.label: Carried-interest holding period
nodes.syn: Three-year holding period for long-term capital gain on certain carried interests.

nodes.label: Publicly traded partnerships
nodes.syn: A publicly traded partnership is taxed as a C-corp unless 90% or more of income is qualifying passive income.

nodes.label: Allocations / SEE safe harbor
nodes.syn: Capital-account maintenance plus the substantial-economic-effect safe harbor for allocations.

nodes.label: Minimum gain / nonrecourse deductions
nodes.syn: Rules for partnership minimum gain and the allocation of nonrecourse deductions.

nodes.label: 704(c) allocation methods
nodes.syn: Traditional, traditional-with-curative, and remedial methods; the ceiling rule.

nodes.label: Disguised-sale general rule
nodes.syn: General disguised-sale test and the two-year presumption.

nodes.label: Disguised-sale exceptions
nodes.syn: Guaranteed payments, preferred returns, operating cash flow, and preformation reimbursements that are not disguised sales.

nodes.label: Liabilities & qualified liabilities
nodes.syn: Liability assumptions, qualified liabilities, and debt-financed distributions; current rules apply to transfers on/after Oct 4, 2019.

nodes.label: Recourse vs nonrecourse definitions
nodes.syn: Defines recourse and nonrecourse liabilities for partnership tax purposes.

nodes.label: Recourse liability allocation
nodes.syn: Allocates recourse liabilities to the partner who bears the economic risk of loss.

nodes.label: Nonrecourse liability allocation
nodes.syn: Three-tier allocation of nonrecourse liabilities: minimum gain, 704(c) gain, then profits.

nodes.label: Hot-asset computation
nodes.syn: Mechanics for computing ordinary income from hot assets on sales and disproportionate distributions.

nodes.label: Marketable securities as money
nodes.syn: Implements §731(c): which securities count and the built-in-gain reduction.

nodes.label: Retirement-payment allocation
nodes.syn: Allocates retiring-partner payments between §736(a) and §736(b).

nodes.label: Org / syndication definitions
nodes.syn: Defines organizational expenses (amortizable) versus syndication expenses (capitalized).

nodes.label: GP-to-LP conversion; unitary basis
nodes.syn: Conversion of a general to a limited partnership is not a taxable exchange; a partner has one unitary basis.

nodes.label: Single-member LLC to partnership
nodes.syn: Converting a disregarded single-member LLC to a partnership: treated as a sale/contribution with basis consequences.

nodes.label: Partnership to disregarded entity
nodes.syn: Converting a partnership to a disregarded entity: deemed liquidating distribution then asset purchase.

nodes.label: Profits interest for services
nodes.syn: Receipt of a partnership profits interest for services is generally not a taxable event.

nodes.label: Nonvested profits interest
nodes.syn: Clarifies profits-interest treatment for substantially nonvested interests.

nodes.label: Partnership existence = intent
nodes.syn: Whether a partnership exists turns on the parties' good-faith intent to join together to carry on a business.

nodes.label: Multi-factor partnership test
nodes.syn: Lists factors (agreement, control over income/capital, shared responsibilities, conduct) for whether a partnership exists.

nodes.label: Debt-financed distribution recast
nodes.syn: A leveraged distribution was recast as a disguised sale where the partner's indemnity lacked economic substance.

nodes.label: Nonrecourse debt in amount realized
nodes.syn: Nonrecourse liability is included in amount realized even if it exceeds the property's value (Crane/Tufts line).

nodes.label: Records & partnership existence
nodes.syn: Inadequate records shift the burden to the taxpayer; an unsubstantiated partnership was treated as a sole proprietorship.

nodes.label: Unrealized receivables
nodes.syn: Rights to payment for goods/services not yet in income; an ordinary-income hot asset under §751.

nodes.label: Inventory items
nodes.syn: Property held for sale plus other non-capital/non-1231 assets; a hot asset under §751.

nodes.label: Retiring partner: other payments
nodes.syn: Payments not for partnership property are a distributive share or a guaranteed payment (ordinary).

nodes.label: Retiring partner: property payments
nodes.syn: Payments for the partner's share of partnership property are liquidating distributions under §731 (capital, except §751(b)).

nodes.label: Distributed-property basis: carryover
nodes.syn: In a current distribution, distributed property takes the partnership's basis (carryover).

nodes.label: Distributed-property basis: cap
nodes.syn: Distributed-property basis cannot exceed outside basis less money distributed in the same distribution.

nodes.label: Distributed-property basis: liquidation
nodes.syn: In liquidation, distributed property takes the partner's remaining outside basis (after money).

nodes.label: Seven-year mixing-bowl (distribution)
nodes.syn: Distributing contributed 704(c) property to another partner within 7 years triggers the contributor's built-in gain or loss.

nodes.label: Foreign-partner built-in gain
nodes.syn: Nonrecognition can be overridden where built-in-gain property is contributed to a partnership with related foreign partners (Treas. Reg. 1.721(c)).

nodes.label: Partner not acting as a partner
nodes.syn: Transactions where a partner deals with the partnership as a non-partner; the statutory anchor for disguised sales.

nodes.label: Related-party loss/character rules
nodes.syn: Disallows losses and recharacterizes gain on sales between a partner and a controlled partnership.

nodes.label: Nonrecourse tier 1: minimum gain
nodes.syn: First tier of nonrecourse allocation: a partner's share of partnership minimum gain.

nodes.label: Nonrecourse tier 2: 704(c) gain
nodes.syn: Second tier: the 704(c) built-in gain the partner would be allocated on a deemed sale.

nodes.label: Nonrecourse tier 3: profits
nodes.syn: Third tier: remaining nonrecourse liabilities by the partner's share of profits.

nodes.label: 704(c) traditional method
nodes.syn: Traditional method; limited by the ceiling rule (tax items capped at actual partnership items).

nodes.label: 704(c) curative method
nodes.syn: Traditional method with curative allocations of other actual items to cure ceiling-rule distortions.

nodes.label: 704(c) remedial method
nodes.syn: Remedial method; creates offsetting notional items to fully cure ceiling-rule distortions.

nodes.label: Disguised-sale exception: guaranteed payment
nodes.syn: Reasonable guaranteed payments for capital are generally not part of a disguised sale.

nodes.label: Disguised-sale exception: preferred return
nodes.syn: Reasonable preferred returns are presumed not part of a disguised sale (with a rate safe harbor).

nodes.label: Disguised-sale exception: operating cash flow
nodes.syn: Operating cash flow distributions within limits are presumed not part of a disguised sale.

nodes.label: Disguised-sale exception: preformation
nodes.syn: Reimbursement of certain preformation capital expenditures is not part of a disguised sale.

nodes.label: One Big Beautiful Bill Act
nodes.syn: [VERIFIED] Signed July 4, 2025. Made 199A permanent, restored EBITDA-based 163(j), and enacted new 1062.

nodes.label: Qualified farmland installment election
nodes.syn: [VERIFIED] OBBBA election to pay the net income tax on gain from a sale of qualified farmland to a qualified farmer in 4 equal annual installments (a payment-deferral mechanism, NOT a gain exclusion); IRS implemented via Form 1062 (Dec. 2025). Effective for sales in tax years beginning after July 4, 2025.

nodes.label: Sec. 1062 farmland election — estimated-tax relief
nodes.syn: [REPORTED] Estimated-tax relief tied to the §1062 farmland installment election: a taxpayer may exclude 75% of the applicable net tax liability from required annual estimated payments (§§6654/6655); penalty relief is automatic for compliant returns or via Form 843 annotated 'Abatement requested pursuant to Notice 2026-3'. Reported via practitioner sources; primary IRB cite to confirm.

nodes.label: CAMT-partnership interim guidance
nodes.syn: [VERIFIED] Issued July 29, 2025 (IRB 2025-34, 316; Aug 18, 2025); partially withdraws the 2024 CAMT proposed regs for partnerships and gives corporate partners simplified AFSI methods. Confirmed still operative by Notice 2026-7.

nodes.label: CAMT top-down election
nodes.syn: [VERIFIED] Lets a corporate partner treat 80% of its own financial-statement income as its distributive share of AFSI, avoiding bottom-up math.

nodes.label: CAMT taxable-income election
nodes.syn: [VERIFIED] A simplified path mapping AFSI to regular taxable income for partners with a small interest.

nodes.label: CAMT full Subchapter K method
nodes.syn: [VERIFIED] Aligns the CAMT treatment of partnership contributions and distributions with regular Subchapter K rules.

nodes.label: CAMT applicable-corporation interim guidance
nodes.syn: [VERIFIED] CAMT interim guidance, IRB 2025-26 (June 23, 2025); part of the stacked interim-notice regime for the corporate AMT (§56A) pending proposed/final regulations. Specific operative content to confirm against the notice.

nodes.label: CAMT additional interim guidance
nodes.syn: [VERIFIED] CAMT interim guidance, IRB 2025-43 (Oct 20, 2025); continues the stacked interim-notice regime for the corporate AMT (§56A). Later modified by Notice 2026-7.

nodes.label: CAMT additional interim guidance
nodes.syn: [VERIFIED] CAMT interim guidance (irs.gov drop n-25-49), issued late 2025 after Notice 2025-46; reiterates that a corporate partner takes into account only its distributive share of partnership AFSI under §56A(c)(2)(D)(i), and that Treasury intends to partially withdraw the Sept 2024 CAMT proposed regs and reissue. Exact IRB cite/date to confirm. Later modified by Notice 2026-7.

nodes.label: CAMT additional interim guidance
nodes.syn: [VERIFIED] Additional CAMT interim guidance (irs.gov drop n-26-07), Part III of the IRB; MODIFIES (does not withdraw) Notices 2025-46 and 2025-49, transition/applicability Feb 18, 2026. Confirms Notice 2025-28 remains operative and that NO final CAMT regulations exist as of early 2026.

nodes.label: CAMT proposed regs — partnership AFSI
nodes.syn: [VERIFIED] CAMT proposed regulations, 89 FR 75062 (Sept 13, 2024), RIN 1545-BQ84. Contains Subchapter-K-specific Prop. §1.56A-5 (a partner's distributive share of partnership AFSI; bottom-up / applicable method) and Prop. §1.56A-20 (applying Part II Subchapter K principles via a deferred-sale method for §721(a) contributions). PROPOSED, not final; Treasury intends to partially withdraw and reissue. The interim notices govern in the meantime.

nodes.label: Partner property-distribution report
nodes.syn: [VERIFIED] TY2024+; any partner receiving a §732 property distribution files a separate Form 7217 per distribution date (money and marketable-securities-as-money excluded).

nodes.label: Basis-shifting economic substance
nodes.syn: [VERIFIED-superseded] Applied the §7701(o) economic substance doctrine to related-party basis-shifting via §§732(b)/734(b)/743(b); REVOKED in 2025 by Notice 2025-34.

nodes.label: Basis-shifting proposed-reg notice
nodes.syn: [VERIFIED-superseded] Announced forthcoming basis-shifting regs; withdrawn by Notice 2025-23 and revoked by Notice 2025-34.

nodes.label: Basis-shifting transaction of interest
nodes.syn: [VERIFIED] Final 1/14/2025 (TD 10028, 90 FR 2972) designating related-party basis-shifting as a reportable transaction of interest. Notice 2025-23 (Apr 2025) announced its removal and waived disclosure penalties, but the section REMAINS CODIFIED in the eCFR (current through 6/12/2026) with no removing Treasury Decision in effect — still on the books, though effectively unenforced. Corrected June 2026 against the official eCFR after the prior seed wrongly recorded a 3/6/2026 removal.

nodes.label: Removes basis-shifting TOI reg
nodes.syn: [VERIFIED] Issued April 17, 2025; announced removal of Treas. Reg. 1.6011-18, waived disclosure penalties, and withdrew Notice 2024-54.

nodes.label: Revokes 2024-14 and 2024-54
nodes.syn: [VERIFIED] 2025 notice revoking Rev. Rul. 2024-14 and Notice 2024-54 with penalty relief.

nodes.label: Clean-energy elect-out of Subch. K
nodes.syn: [VERIFIED] Final regs (late 2024) let qualifying unincorporated clean-energy co-ownership arrangements elect out of Subchapter K to ease §6417 direct-pay credit collection.

nodes.label: Elective (direct) payment of credits
nodes.syn: [VERIFIED] IRA-2022 elective/direct-pay mechanism for certain energy credits; interacts with the §761(a) clean-energy elect-out.

nodes.label: Cross-border DPL tracking
nodes.syn: [REPORTED] Final 2025 regs on disregarded payment losses tied to the dual-consolidated-loss regime (§1503(d)); a reported sunset via Notice 2025-44 folding DPL into DCL logic is UNCONFIRMED — verify the notice number.

nodes.label: AI-assisted LPC audit selection
nodes.syn: [CONTEXT] IRS Large Partnership Compliance program using ML to select large, multi-tiered passthroughs for audit; an enforcement program, not legal authority. A 'Rapid LPC Field Audit' expansion is reported to have stalled on budget/staffing — UNCONFIRMED.

nodes.label: K-2/K-3 small-partnership relief
nodes.syn: [CONTEXT] Domestic filing exception and partner-notification rules let qualifying small/domestic partnerships skip K-2/K-3; specific 2025 threshold and 'Part XII QDD' changes UNCONFIRMED — verify against current instructions.

edges.m: loss allowed only to extent of basis
transferee step-up/down on sale of interest
SEE safe harbor keyed to capital accounts
partially withdraws the 2024 CAMT proposed regs for partnerships
Prop. 1.56A-5 partner distributive share of AFSI
estimated-tax relief for the 1062 farmland election
announced removal & waived disclosure penalties; section still codified per eCFR 6/12/2026
